content.full_text:
Law Offices of
LAWRENCE A. HAUN
Eleven and One-half South Washington Street
Post Office Box 1056
Sonora, California 95370

Lawrence A. Haun
Roger A. Brown

Telephone (209) 532-1958    Telecopier (209) 532-1963

January 8, 1991

Gregory W. Baugher
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
428 J. Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: FPPC No. 88/239

Dear Mr. Baugher:

This is to request Commission advice concerning my duties under the Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended. This request is in part made pursuant to Government Code section 83114 (b). It is also addressed to your authority as Executive Director, Chief of Staff and Presiding Officer of the Probable Cause Conference.

As you know, I was formerly Chief of the Enforcement Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission. I resigned that position in July 1988, and left the Commission in October 1988. I have been consulted by the respondents in enforcement case number 88/239 concerning whether I may represent them in these proceedings.

Please advise me in writing as soon as possible whether:

a) You will grant the respondents a continuance of the Probable Cause Conference currently scheduled for January 15, 1991, to enable them to secure counsel to represent them;

b) Government Code sections 87400 and 87401 would prohibit me from representing these respondents for compensation in this enforcement proceeding; and

c) The FPPC will agree not to move to recuse me on the basis of my prior employment if the respondents choose to retain me following your advice on the preceding questions.

Gregory W. Baugher
January 8, 1991
Page 2

I have discussed with Acting Enforcement Chief, Janis
McLean, whether the FPPC enforcement file contains any indication
of involvement by me. She said it does not.

The initial complaint was apparently received by the
Commission on or about June 9, 1988. The enforcement division's
intake unit acknowledged receipt of the complaint by form letter
on or about June 14, 1988. According to Ms. McLean, the next
activity recorded in the file is dated April 4, 1989, long after
I left State service.

I have absolutely no recollection of this case. I have no
confidential information about the case acquired by virtue of my
prior employment. I have no recollection of ever participating
in or making any decisions or recommendations regarding this case
while employed by the State. I can recall, as a State
employee, ever taking part personally or substantially through
decision, approval, disapproval, formal written recommendation,
rendering advice, investigation or the acquisition or use of
confidential information concerning this case.

I understand that Ms. McLean has no knowledge of any
involvement by me as a State employee with this matter. In
addition, I understand Ms. McLean has consulted with Chief
Investigator, Al Herndon, who also recalls no involvement by
me. I encourage you to confer with other present or past employees of
the Commission to determine whether anyone else has any
recollection of any involvement by me with this matter.

The respondents in this matter have asked me to represent
them. I am satisfied that I did not "participate" in this matter
within the meaning of Government Code sections 87400 and 87401.
However, the Commission has the records and files from this
period 2 1/2 years ago. As a result, you are in a better
position to determine the facts than I am. Moreover, the
respondents do not want their attorney disqualified late in the
proceedings after they have invested substantial sums in their
defense. This result would unfairly penalize them, especially if
they are ultimately found to have done nothing wrong.

In your position as Executive Director, you have the
authority to grant a continuance and to control the course of
litigation. I therefore request a continuance of the Probable
Cause Conference until the respondents' choice of counsel can be
resolved and counsel has time to become familiar with the case.

It is also in your role as Executive Director that I ask you
to waive any right the Commission might have to challenge my
representation of these respondents on the basis of my former
employment. This assumes that you find I did not "participate"

Gregory W. Baugher
January 8, 1991
Page 3

in this matter as a state employee within the meaning of
Government Code section 87400-87401. If you are satisfied, as I
am, that I have no confidential information obtained as a state
employee and that I never "participated" in this matter within
the meaning of the statute, there would be no prejudice to the
State from this waiver.

The respondents are individuals of modest means who need the
assurance that you would not try to recuse me after they have
invested significant sums in their defense. The most equitable
procedure would be to resolve this question before I am retained,
not after.

You control the evidence and are in the best position to
advise me, in advance, whether I am required to decline these
respondents' request. I have disclosed all material facts of
which I am aware, and I am seeking your advice in a good faith
effort to determine my duties under the Political Reform Act.

Thank you for your consideration of these requests.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Roger A. Brown

RAB:sj/212.3/013

sections.question: a) Will you grant the respondents a continuance of the Probable Cause Conference currently scheduled for January 15, 1991, to enable them to secure counsel to represent them? b) Would Government Code sections 87400 and 87401 prohibit me from representing these respondents for compensation in this enforcement proceeding? c) Will the FPPC agree not to move to recuse me on the basis of my prior employment if the respondents choose to retain me following your advice on the preceding questions?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester, a former FPPC Enforcement Division chief, seeks advice on whether he can represent respondents in an enforcement case (88/239) without violating Government Code sections 87400-87401, and requests a continuance and waiver of any FPPC motion to recuse based on his prior employment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers are present. The numbered requests (a, b, c) form the substantive questions. The letter includes factual background about the requester's prior employment and absence of involvement in the case. Document ID references FPPC No. 88/239 (the enforcement case in question).

embedding.summary: Lawrence A. Haun, former Chief of the FPPC Enforcement Division (resigned July 1988), requests written advice from FPPC Executive Director Gregory Baugher regarding whether he may represent respondents in enforcement case 88/239 without violating conflict of interest statutes, and requests a continuance and advance waiver of any recusal motion.
